$comment: Generalized SRP (Single-Responsibility Package) contract, derived from FLU V12 architecture. All FLU-family packages must conform to this schema.
derived_from: FLU V15 — Fractal Logic Unit / Phased Fractal Number Theory

philosophy.core_principle: Every package in the FLU family solves ONE mathematical problem rigorously. It does not grow by accretion — it grows by crystallisation.
philosophy.epistemic_discipline: Every claim is either PROVEN (with proof), CONJECTURE (with empirical support), or DESIGN_INTENT (with rationale). There are no unmarked assumptions.
philosophy.separation_of_concerns: Mathematics is never polluted by application logic. Theory layer has no imports from applications layer.
philosophy.single_source_of_truth: Every mathematical claim appears in exactly one canonical location (the theorem registry). All documentation, tests, and code reference that location.
philosophy.honest_benchmarks: No benchmark claims a result that was not actually measured. Every claim includes the conditions under which it holds and the conditions under which it does not.

core.purpose: Algorithms and data transformations. No theory imports.
core.naming: Noun or verb describing the operation (fm_dance, factoradic, lo_shu).
constraints: All public functions documented with complexity claims

theory.purpose: Formal theorem statements, proofs, verification functions.
theory.naming: theory_{domain}.py for domain theories, theorem_registry.py for the central registry.
constraints: All theorems MUST have status: PROVEN | CONJECTURE | DESIGN_INTENT
Verification functions MUST be runnable and deterministic

container.purpose: Algebraic container operations — composition, combination, identity.
container.note: Required if the package defines composable container objects.

utils.purpose: Benchmarks, formatters, validators, CLI entry points.
constraints: benchmarks.py MUST document honest criteria (not aspirational)
All complexity claims MUST have a corresponding benchmark function

applications.purpose: Domain-specific use of the core. Imports from core and theory; NOT vice versa.

required.run_all.py: Runs all tests. Final line reports N/N passing.
required.test_core/: Tests for correctness of core algorithms.
required.test_theory/: Tests that verify theorem conditions computationally.
required.benchmarks/: Runnable benchmark scripts (not just assertions, but measurements).

standards.minimum_coverage: All public functions in core/ have at least one test.
standards.theorem_linkage: Each theorem test references the theorem ID in its docstring.
standards.no_hidden_failures: Tests never catch exceptions silently. Failures must propagate.

required_files.README.md: Package overview, badges (theorem score, test score), quick-start.
required_files.THEOREMS.md: Human-readable theorem registry with full proof sketches.
required_files.BENCHMARKS.md: Live benchmark results. Proposed vs delivered is documented honestly.
required_files.OPEN_DEBT.md: Every known defect, proof gap, and data gap with resolution path.
required_files.CHANGELOG.md: All theorem status changes and breaking API changes.
required_files.PERSPECTIVES.md: Optional. For packages with multiple equivalent mathematical views.

optional_files.ARCHITECTURE.md: Module map, dependency rules, naming conventions.
optional_files.ROADMAP_Vn.md: Next sprint plan with acceptance criteria.
optional_files.FLU_MANIFEST.json: Machine-readable public entry point (see manifest schema below).

required_files.README.md: Top-level README. Badges: tests, theorems. 5-line summary.

required_fields_per_theorem.name: Human-readable name: '{ID} -- {Short Description}'
required_fields_per_theorem.proof: For PROVEN: complete proof sketch. For CONJECTURE: empirical evidence and open questions.
required_fields_per_theorem.conditions: List of conditions under which the theorem holds.
required_fields_per_theorem.references: List of code refs, doc refs, literature refs.

status_definitions.PROVEN: Complete mathematical argument exists AND computationally verified for stated domain.
status_definitions.CONJECTURE: Empirically supported across tested domain. Formal proof incomplete.
status_definitions.DESIGN_INTENT: Design property, not a mathematical theorem. Verified by construction.
status_definitions.FALSE: Disproven. Kept as named negative result.

theorem_registry_spec.machine_readable_export: THEOREM_REGISTRY.json — auto-generated from Python registry (see tools/generate_registry_json.py)

required_fields_per_benchmark.validates: The theorem ID(s) or complexity claim being validated.
required_fields_per_benchmark.honest_criteria: The ACTUAL pass/fail threshold, not an aspirational one.
required_fields_per_benchmark.proposed_vs_delivered: If the benchmark differs from an earlier proposal, explain why honestly.
required_fields_per_benchmark.conditions: Hardware, Python version, parameter range where the claim holds.
required_fields_per_benchmark.pass_or_fail: Returns a boolean result, not just a measurement.

prohibited: Aspirational benchmarks that have never been run.
Benchmarks that only print numbers without a pass/fail criterion.
Benchmarks that silently cap their parameters to make claims easier to satisfy.
Phantom benchmarks measuring functions that do not exist in the codebase.

changelog_entry_required_for: Any API removal or signature change

epistemic_discipline.no_unmarked_assumptions: Every assumption that is not self-evident must be stated explicitly.
epistemic_discipline.no_speculative_naming_without_implementa: A function or class cannot be named after a concept that has no implementation. Names are not claims.
epistemic_discipline.conjecture_downgrade_policy: If a PROVEN theorem is found to have a gap, it is immediately downgraded to CONJECTURE and logged in OPEN_DEBT.md.
epistemic_discipline.honest_benchmarks_policy: The proposed criterion and the delivered criterion are both stated. If they differ, the reason is given.
epistemic_discipline.open_debt_is_not_shame: OPEN_DEBT.md is a sign of maturity, not failure. Every research package has open debt. The discipline is in tracking it.

public_api_spec.theorem_linkage_in_docstring: Functions that implement or verify a theorem must reference the theorem ID in their docstring.
public_api_spec.no_internal_leakage: Private implementation details (prefixed _) are never re-exported in __init__.py.

FLU_MANIFEST.json.purpose: Public entry point for LLMs, auditors, and external packages.
should_contain: all theorems (compact: id, status, one-line statement)
all public functions grouped by role

THEOREM_REGISTRY.json.purpose: Machine-queryable theorem database with dependency graph.
THEOREM_REGISTRY.json.generation: Auto-generated by tools/generate_registry_json.py — never edited by hand.
should_contain: all theorem records with full proof sketches

compliance_checklist.item: docs/BENCHMARKS.md has at least one benchmark with honest criterion
README.md has theorem badge and test badge
docs/PERSPECTIVES.md exists if multiple equivalent views exist
Each benchmark returns a boolean pass/fail result
